title: Electric Arc Furnace (EAF)
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Electric Arc Furnace (EAF), the primary melting unit used in stainless steel manufacturing. It covers furnace construction, working principle, charging process, melting operations, tapping, safety measures, and quality control.
learningObjectives: Understand the purpose of the Electric Arc Furnace.
Identify the major components of an EAF.
Recognize important safety practices during EAF operation.

content.introduction: The Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) is the first major production unit in stainless steel manufacturing. It melts scrap steel and alloy materials using electrical energy to produce molten steel for further refining.
content.purpose: The EAF converts solid metallic raw materials into molten steel while achieving the required temperature for refining.
content.workingPrinciple: Electric current passes through graphite electrodes to generate an electric arc. The intense heat produced by the arc melts the scrap and alloy materials inside the furnace.
advantages: Lower environmental impact compared to blast furnaces
safetyPrecautions: Maintain safe distance from molten metal.
Never stand near the tapping area during tapping.
Ensure cooling water system is functioning properly.
Follow Lock-Out Tag-Out (LOTO) procedures during maintenance.
content.summary: The Electric Arc Furnace is the heart of stainless steel melting operations. Proper control of melting parameters, power input, raw material quality, and safety practices ensures efficient production of high-quality molten steel.
